socrates.text:
": "cat > /app/crop_extract.py << 'EOF'
import os
import subprocess
import re
import tempfile
from PIL import Image

# Focus on frames from /app/frames (1fps)
frames = sorted([f for f in os.listdir('/app/frames') if f.endswith('.png')])
print(f"Processing {len(frames)} frames...")

# First frame to get image dimensions
sample = Image.open(os.path.join('/app/frames', frames[0]))
w, h = sample.size
print(f"Image size: {w}x{h}")

# Crop bottom portion (command prompt area) - bottom

20% of image
crop_top = int(h * 0.75)

prev_text = ""
for i, frame in enumerate(frames):
    frame_path = os.path.join('/app/frames', frame)
    img = Image.open(frame_path)
    
    # Crop bottom portion
    cropped = img.crop((0, crop_top, w, h))
    
    # Save to temp and OCR
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.png', delete=False) as tmp:
        tmp_path = tmp.name
        cropped.save(tmp_path)
    
    result = subprocess.run(['tesseract', tmp_path, 'stdout'], 
                         capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10)
    os.unlink

(tmp_path)
    
    text = result.stdout.strip()
    if text != prev_text:  # Only print when text changes
        print(f"\n=== Frame {i+1} ===")
        print(text)
        prev_text = text

EOF
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 /app/crop_extract.py 2>&1 | tee /app/crop_log.txt
",
      "duration": 120.0
    }
  ]
}

{
  "analysis": "The output is getting mixed up. Let me take a more systematic approach and create a cleaner extraction script that focuses on the bottom of each frame where the command prompt appears.",
  "plan": "1. Create a script to crop just the bottom portion of each frame (where the command prompt is). 2. Run OCR on just that portion. 3. Track when the prompt text changes to identify new commands.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > /app/crop_extract.py << 'EOF'
import os
import subprocess
import re
import tempfile
from PIL import Image

# Focus on frames from /app/frames (1fps)
frames = sorted([f for f in os.listdir('/app/frames') if f.endswith('.png')])
print(f"Processing {len(frames)} frames...")

# First frame to get image dimensions
sample = Image.open(os.path.join('/app/frames', frames[0]))
w, h = sample.size
print(f"Image size: {w}x{h}")

# Crop bottom portion (command prompt area) - bottom 20% of image
crop_top = int(h * 0.75)

prev_text = ""
for i, frame in enumerate(frames):
    frame_path = os.path.join('/app/frames', frame)
    img = Image.open(frame_path)
    
    # Crop bottom portion
    cropped = img.crop((0, crop_top, w, h))
    
    # Save to temp and OCR
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.png', delete=False) as tmp:
        tmp_path = tmp.name
        cropped.save(tmp_path)
    
    result = subprocess.run(['tesseract', tmp_path, 'stdout'], 
                         capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10)
    os.unlink(tmp_path)
    
    text = result.stdout.strip()
    if text != prev_text:  # Only print when text changes
        print(f"\n=== Frame {i+1} ===")
        print(text)
        prev_text = text

EOF
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 /app/crop_extract.py 2>&1 | tee /app/crop_log.txt
",

    }
  ]
}
